rule_name: User Couldn't Call a Privileged Service 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess'

text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=4673 AND "Service Name"='LsaRegisterLogonProcess()' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%0x8010000000000000%' AQL filter query

_$.description: Converted from SIGMA Rule.  Original Author: Roberto Rodriguez (source), Ilyas Ochkov (rule), oscd.community SIGMA Title: User Couldn't Call a Privileged Service 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess' SIGMA Description: The 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess' function verifies that the application making the function call is a logon process by checking that it has the SeTcbPrivilege privilege set. Possible Rubeus tries to get a handle to LSA.  False positives: - Unknown  This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
_$.name: User Couldn't Call a Privileged Service 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess'
